description: Two items got their applied state preserved in the working tree but lost their worklist tracking when a later proposal overwrote the file. Re-adding for the commit-approval step.

items.before: generate_worklist_changelog() classified each diff as proposed / advanced / renamed / pruned, where 'advanced' was the status-change bucket and 'pruned' lumped committed/dropped together. 'renamed' was dead since rename_from was removed in 67eff19. Per user feedback: the relevant phases are proposed → applied → committed (and dropped as an alternate outcome). Per follow-up 'also drop pruned' directive: unauthorized removals are already blocked by maybe_enforce_worklist_policy upstream, so the pruned fallback was theoretical.
items.after: Rewrote generate_worklist_changelog: takes AppHandle now; reads resources/.worklist-authorization.json once at the top to classify removals; renamed advanced → applied; removed renamed and pruned buckets entirely; new four-way taxonomy is proposed / applied / committed / dropped. Summary line joins only non-empty buckets. Suppress condition updated to check the new buckets. Removed the rename_from lookup and renamed_olds HashSet. Section headers updated to match. Re-adding as applied because the original worklist tracking got overwritten before commit approval landed.
